meta.title: ENT Practice Software | Audiology Coordination & In-Office Procedures | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to ENT practice operations. Audiology and imaging coordination. In-office procedure optimization. Allergy program management. Hearing aid tracking.

hero.subhead: Your audiology testing needs coordination. Your in-office procedures need efficiency. Your allergy program needs management. clinIQ coordinates ENT testing, optimizes procedures, and manages complex service lines.

sections.content:
ENT practices run multiple service lines simultaneously: clinic visits, audiology, in-office procedures, surgical scheduling, and often allergy services.

**The testing coordination problem:**

Audiograms, tympanometry, vestibular testing, CT sinuses, flexible laryngoscopy — testing generates significant volume. Results need to be available before clinical visits.

**The procedure efficiency opportunity:**

ENT performs many in-office procedures: tube placement, balloon sinuplasty, in-office laryngoscopy, biopsies, allergy injections. Each requires room setup, equipment, and provider time.

**The allergy service complexity:**

Allergy testing, immunotherapy mixing, injection scheduling, reaction monitoring — allergy programs have their own operational requirements.

**The surgical coordination:**

ENT surgery spans outpatient (tubes, tonsils, septoplasty) to complex (sinus surgery, head and neck oncology). Each has different authorization requirements.

Audiology testing is core to ENT practice but often poorly coordinated with physician visits.

**What clinIQ does:**

Test tracking pipeline: Audiogram ordered → scheduled → completed → reviewed → available for visit.

Pre-visit integration: When patient arrives for ENT visit, audiology results visible and reviewed.

Hearing aid coordination: For patients proceeding to hearing aids — fitting, adjustment, follow-up tracked.

Vestibular testing: VNG, rotary chair, VEMP — ordered and tracked through completion.

**The efficiency gain:**

ENT sees patient, audiogram not done or not available, visit is partially wasted. With coordination, every ENT visit has necessary audiology data.

ENT performs high volumes of in-office procedures:

- Flexible laryngoscopy
- Nasal endoscopy
- Ear procedures (tube placement, wax removal, biopsies)
- Balloon sinuplasty
- In-office excisions
- Allergy testing

**What clinIQ does:**

Procedure room visibility: Room available, occupied, turnover. Equipment status.

Procedure scheduling: Duration-appropriate scheduling. Balloon sinuplasty needs different time than cerumen removal.

Equipment tracking: Scopes, procedure trays, specialized equipment — availability confirmed.

Consent and preparation: Pre-procedure requirements verified before scheduling.

ENT allergy programs involve testing, immunotherapy, and ongoing injection management.

**Allergy testing:**
- Skin prick testing scheduling
- Results documentation
- Immunotherapy formulation

**Injection program:**
- Injection scheduling (weekly, biweekly, monthly)
- Dose tracking and escalation
- Reaction documentation
- Vial expiration tracking

**What clinIQ does:**

Injection scheduling: Patient injection schedule visible. Missed injections flagged.

Dose tracking: Current dose, escalation protocol, next dose.

Vial management: Vial expiration tracking. Remix scheduling.

Reaction documentation: Post-injection monitoring documented.

ENT surgery ranges from quick outpatient procedures to complex oncologic resections.

**What clinIQ does:**

Pre-op coordination: Labs, clearance, imaging, consent — tracked per case.

Authorization tracking: Sinus surgery, septoplasty, tonsillectomy — auth status visible.

Post-op tracking: Post-operative visits scheduled and tracked.

**Week 1-2: Configuration and Training**

Audiology coordination, procedure scheduling, allergy program configured. Staff trained.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: Testing coordination immediate. Procedure visibility active.

Month 3: Measurable efficiency improvements. Allergy program organized.

**ROI Math:**

Procedure efficiency: 1-2 additional procedures/day × revenue = significant annual impact

Allergy program: Systematic management improves retention and compliance

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

faqs.q: Does this work with audiology practices?
faqs.a: Yes. Whether audiology is in-house or referred, testing coordination applies.

faqs.q: How do you track allergy injections?
faqs.a: Injection schedule per patient. Dose escalation protocol. Vial expiration. Reaction documentation.
Hearing aid fitting, adjustment, and follow-up appointments tracked.
